docs.title: Drone Battery Monte Carlo
docs.text:
This project compares drone battery options using a Monte Carlo simulation model under varying mission conditions (e.g., payload, wind, and temperature). It includes:

  • A simulation engine that estimates energy use and mission success
  • A desktop GUI for running comparisons and viewing plots
  • Published API documentation generated by Doxygen

docs.title: Technical API
docs.text:
![GUI screenshot](./assets/gui/params.png

Open the generated Doxygen documentation here: ➡️ Doxygen API Reference

docs.text: - API docs: https://jmo-ops.github.io/drone-battery-monte-carlo/ - GUI page: GUI (use the navigation menu) - Logic/model page: Logic (use the navigation menu)

docs.title: 1) Create / activate your environment
docs.text:
If you already have a .venv, activate it:

```bash ..venv\Scripts\activate

docs.title: Technical API
docs.text: ➡️ Open the Doxygen API Reference

docs.title: GUI
docs.text: The project includes a desktop GUI (Tkinter + matplotlib) that lets you run Monte Carlo simulations and compare two battery configurations across typical and extreme mission conditions.

docs.title: 1) Simulation
docs.text:
Run the standard Monte Carlo simulation to compare Battery A vs Battery B under nominal sampling assumptions. The GUI produces:

  • Text summary of results (success rate, remaining energy stats, operational averages)
  • Distribution plots (remaining energy histograms)

docs.title: 2) Parameters Summary
docs.text: Shows the active parameters and model settings used during the run (battery parameters, mission config, penalties, and sampling ranges).

docs.title: 3) Extreme Conditions
docs.text: Runs a deliberately biased Monte Carlo intended to stress the mission (high wind, low temperature, high payload, and slightly longer distance).

docs.title: Extreme simulation settings
docs.text:
• Number of runs per battery (example shown: 500)
  • Biases:
  • Wind: high-biased (10–20 mph)
  • Temp: low-biased (10–95 °F)
  • Payload: high-biased (2.5–5 lb)
  • Distance factor: high-biased (1.0–1.1)

docs.title: Example results (Extreme Conditions)
docs.text: Below is an example output captured from the GUI.

docs.title: Li-ion (NMC, cylindrical) — EXTREME Monte Carlo Results
docs.text:
• Total simulation trials: 500
  • Successful mission trials (met reserve requirement): 500 / 500 (100.0% success probability)

Remaining Energy Distribution - Mean remaining energy: 32.1% - 5th percentile remaining: 16.5% - 95th percentile remaining: 46.8%

Operational Averages - Avg miles (all trials): 16.01 mi - Avg payload (all trials): 4.16 lb - Avg miles (successful only): 16.01 mi - Avg payload (successful only): 4.16 lb

Energy Use & Deliveries - Avg energy used per mission: 168.3 Wh - Missions per charge (float): 1.60 - Approx. deliveries per full charge (floor): 1

Extreme input bias - Wind: high-biased (10–20 mph) - Temp: low-biased (10–95 °F) - Payload: high-biased (2.5–5 lb) - Distance factor: high-biased (1.0–1.1)

docs.title: LiPo (pouch, high-power) — EXTREME Monte Carlo Results
docs.text:
• Total simulation trials: 500
  • Successful mission trials (met reserve requirement): 500 / 500 (100.0% success probability)

Remaining Energy Distribution - Mean remaining energy: 29.4% - 5th percentile remaining: 16.7% - 95th percentile remaining: 42.1%

Operational Averages - Avg miles (all trials): 16.01 mi - Avg payload (all trials): 4.18 lb - Avg miles (successful only): 16.01 mi - Avg payload (successful only): 4.18 lb

Energy Use & Deliveries - Avg energy used per mission: 158.3 Wh - Missions per charge (float): 1.48 - Approx. deliveries per full charge (floor): 1

Extreme input bias - Wind: high-biased (10–20 mph) - Temp: low-biased (10–95 °F) - Payload: high-biased (2.5–5 lb) - Distance factor: high-biased (1.0–1.1)

docs.title: Li-ion vs LiPo comparison (Extreme)
docs.text:
• Δ Mean remaining (Li-ion - LiPo): +2.66%
  • Δ 5th pct remaining (Li-ion - LiPo): -0.14%
  • Δ 95th pct remaining (Li-ion - LiPo): +4.70%
  • Δ Avg Wh/mission (Li-ion - LiPo): +9.94 Wh
  • Δ Missions/charge (Li-ion - LiPo): +0.13
  • Success rate A: 100.0% | Success rate B: 100.0%

Interpretation (for this run): - Both batteries met the reserve requirement in all trials. - Li-ion shows a slightly higher average and upper-tail remaining energy under extreme-biased sampling. - LiPo used slightly less energy per mission in this run, but had lower remaining-energy margin overall.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
• If plots don’t appear, verify Tkinter and matplotlib are installed and you’re running in a desktop environment.
  • If the GUI opens but buttons do nothing, run from a terminal to capture tracebacks: ```bash python batteryV2.py

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text: This page captures the system/model requirements for the Drone Battery Monte Carlo model and how each requirement is evaluated.

docs.title: REQ-001 — Payload Weight Impact
docs.text:
Requirement The model shall be able to determine the impact of battery draw on variable payload (0–5 lbs).

Assumptions / Constraints - Drone follows a fixed course. - Payload varies between 0–5 lbs; all other variables held constant. - Assume 0 lbs payload for return trips in battery calculations.

Question(s) Answered - How much does package weight impact battery draw?

Primary Variable - Payload Weight

Method - Baseline System Draw and Mass Penalty - Run X randomly generated runs with payload weight as the only variable to determine impact. - Monte Carlo analysis

Outputs / Acceptance Evidence - Payload Weight impact in terms of battery draw (e.g., watts, Wh, or voltage draw — use whichever your model reports consistently)

Validation Data Source - Battery Test Data Validation

Analysis Deliverable - Plot/table of draw vs. payload weight (0–5 lbs), with summary statistics (mean, min/max, percentile bands)

docs.title: REQ-002 — Flight Distance Impact
docs.text:
Requirement The model shall be able to determine total trip battery draw over variable distance (1–15 miles roundtrip).

Assumptions / Constraints - Drone flies between 1 and 15 miles per trip (roundtrip). - Battery draw across all subsystems remains constant (other than distance-driven energy usage).

Question(s) Answered - How many trips can the drone take on a single charge?

Primary Variable - Flight Distance

Method - Baseline System Draw and Mass Penalty - Run X randomly generated runs with flight distance as the only variable to determine impact. - Monte Carlo analysis

Outputs / Acceptance Evidence - Flight Distance impact in terms of battery draw (e.g., Wh consumed per trip / voltage draw) - Estimated trips per charge across the range

Validation Data Source - End-to-End Simulation

Analysis Deliverable - Plot/table of draw vs. flight distance (1–15 mi RT), plus computed trips-per-charge bands

docs.title: REQ-003 — Trips Before Battery End-of-Life (EOL)
docs.text:
Requirement The model shall be able to determine the total number of trips achievable for each candidate battery before end-of-life (EOL).

Assumptions / Constraints - Drone flies between 1 and 15 miles per trip. - Battery performance degrades with recharge cycles per the candidate battery specification / life-cycle model.

Question(s) Answered - Which candidate battery provides the greatest number of trips before EOL?

Primary Variable - N/A (comparative evaluation across batteries using their specs and recharge/lifecycle model)

Method - Battery specifications + number of recharges / lifecycle model - Run X randomly generated runs with different random inputs to determine which battery delivers the most trips - Monte Carlo analysis

Outputs / Acceptance Evidence - Total number of trips before battery EOL (per candidate battery) - Ranked comparison + confidence bands

Validation Data Source - Life Cycle Test Data

Analysis Deliverable - Comparison chart/table per battery: expected trips-to-EOL, percentiles, and sensitivity notes

docs.title: Requirements Traceability
docs.text: Requirement Primary Variable Method Validation Source Key Output REQ-001 Payload weight (0–5 lbs) Monte Carlo (payload only variable) Battery test data Draw vs payload REQ-002 Flight distance (1–15 mi RT) Monte Carlo (distance only variable) End-to-end simulation Draw vs distance, trips/charge REQ-003 Battery candidate + lifecycle Monte Carlo (battery comparison) Life cycle test data Trips-to-EOL by battery
